content_en:
The analytical method visible in al-Ilal reflects ad-Daraqutni's extraordinary preparation: decades of collecting, memorizing, and studying hadith chains across the full range of the classical tradition. His methodology can be reconstructed from his recorded analyses, and understanding it illuminates both the sophistication of classical hadith criticism and the specific contribution al-Ilal makes to the tradition.

Ad-Daraqutni's primary method was systematic comparison of variants. Before declaring an illah in a chain, he would gather all available transmission variants of the hadith in question — the different chains through which it was reported, the textual variations in different collections, the differences in the identification of specific narrators. This comparative gathering required knowledge of where to look: in which collections the hadith appeared, under which narrators it might be found, and what other scholars had said about it. Ad-Daraqutni's encyclopedic knowledge of the corpus made this gathering possible.

With the variants assembled, he would analyze them to identify which was the most likely original. Several principles governed this analysis. The principle that stronger narrators are more authoritative than weaker ones — when a reliable narrator's chain contradicts a less reliable one, the reliable narrator's version is preferred. The principle of chain elevation — when one variant has a shorter chain connecting to the Prophet and another has a longer one, the longer chain is often a later elaboration of the shorter one. The principle of textual correspondence — when one chain variant matches a known authentic hadith and another adds material not found elsewhere, the additional material is suspect.

His knowledge of the transmission habits of specific narrators was crucial. Some narrators were known to transmit from a specific teacher correctly in some narrations but to confuse chains in others. Some narrators had reliable memories in their prime but became confused in old age. Knowing these individual profiles allowed ad-Daraqutni to assess whether a particular variant from a particular narrator was likely to be accurate or confused.

The question-and-answer format of much of al-Ilal suggests that many of its analyses originated in oral teaching sessions where students posed questions about specific hadith and ad-Daraqutni provided his assessments. This pedagogical origin gives the text a vivid, direct quality that a more formally composed treatise might lack.
content_id:
Metode analitis yang terlihat dalam al-'Ilal mencerminkan persiapan luar biasa ad-Daraqutni: puluhan tahun mengumpulkan, menghafal, dan mempelajari sanad-sanad hadis dari seluruh cakupan tradisi klasik. Metodologinya dapat direkonstruksi dari analisis-analisisnya yang tercatat, dan memahaminya menerangi baik kecanggihan kritik hadis klasik maupun kontribusi spesifik al-'Ilal terhadap tradisi tersebut.

Metode utama ad-Daraqutni adalah perbandingan varian secara sistematis. Sebelum menyatakan 'illah dalam suatu sanad, ia akan mengumpulkan semua varian periwayatan yang tersedia dari hadis yang bersangkutan — sanad-sanad berbeda yang melaluinya hadis diriwayatkan, variasi teks dalam berbagai koleksi, perbedaan dalam identifikasi perawi-perawi tertentu. Pengumpulan komparatif ini memerlukan pengetahuan tentang di mana mencarinya: dalam koleksi apa hadis itu muncul, di bawah perawi mana ia mungkin ditemukan, dan apa yang dikatakan ulama-ulama lain tentangnya. Pengetahuan ensiklopedis ad-Daraqutni tentang korpus memungkinkan pengumpulan ini.

Setelah varian-varian terkumpul, ia akan menganalisisnya untuk mengidentifikasi mana yang kemungkinan besar merupakan yang asli. Beberapa prinsip mengatur analisis ini. Prinsip bahwa perawi yang lebih kuat lebih otoritatif daripada yang lebih lemah — ketika sanad perawi terpercaya bertentangan dengan yang kurang terpercaya, versi perawi terpercaya yang didahulukan. Prinsip elevasi sanad — ketika satu varian memiliki sanad yang lebih pendek yang terhubung ke Nabi dan varian lain memiliki sanad yang lebih panjang, sanad yang lebih panjang seringkali merupakan elaborasi belakangan dari yang lebih pendek. Prinsip kesesuaian teks — ketika satu varian sanad sesuai dengan hadis sahih yang diketahui dan varian lain menambahkan materi yang tidak ditemukan di tempat lain, materi tambahan itu patut dicurigai.

Pengetahuannya tentang kebiasaan periwayatan perawi-perawi tertentu sangat krusial. Beberapa perawi diketahui meriwayatkan dari guru tertentu dengan benar dalam beberapa riwayat namun mengacaukan sanad dalam riwayat-riwayat lain. Beberapa perawi memiliki ingatan yang andal di masa prima mereka namun menjadi kacau di usia tua. Mengetahui profil-profil individual ini memungkinkan ad-Daraqutni menilai apakah varian tertentu dari perawi tertentu kemungkinan akurat atau kacau.

Format tanya-jawab dari sebagian besar al-'Ilal menunjukkan bahwa banyak analisisnya berasal dari sesi pengajaran lisan di mana para murid mengajukan pertanyaan tentang hadis-hadis tertentu dan ad-Daraqutni memberikan penilaiannya. Asal-usul pedagogis ini memberikan teks tersebut kualitas yang hidup dan langsung, yang mungkin tidak dimiliki oleh sebuah risalah yang disusun secara lebih formal.
content_ar:
The analytical method visible in al-Ilal reflects ad-Daraqutni's extraordinary preparation: decades of collecting, memorizing, and studying hadith chains across the full range of the classical tradition. His methodology can be reconstructed from his recorded analyses, and understanding it illuminates both the sophistication of classical hadith criticism and the specific contribution al-Ilal makes to the tradition.

Ad-Daraqutni's primary method was systematic comparison of variants. Before declaring an illah in a chain, he would gather all available transmission variants of the hadith in question — the different chains through which it was reported, the textual variations in different collections, the differences in the identification of specific narrators. This comparative gathering required knowledge of where to look: in which collections the hadith appeared, under which narrators it might be found, and what other scholars had said about it. Ad-Daraqutni's encyclopedic knowledge of the corpus made this gathering possible.

With the variants assembled, he would analyze them to identify which was the most likely original. Several principles governed this analysis. The principle that stronger narrators are more authoritative than weaker ones — when a reliable narrator's chain contradicts a less reliable one, the reliable narrator's version is preferred. The principle of chain elevation — when one variant has a shorter chain connecting to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم and another has a longer one, the longer chain is often a later elaboration of the shorter one. The principle of textual correspondence — when one chain variant matches a known authentic hadith and another adds material not found elsewhere, the additional material is suspect.

His knowledge of the transmission habits of specific narrators was crucial. Some narrators were known to transmit from a specific teacher correctly in some narrations but to confuse chains in others. Some narrators had reliable memories in their prime but became confused in old age. Knowing these individual profiles allowed ad-Daraqutni to assess whether a particular variant from a particular narrator was likely to be accurate or confused.

The question-and-answer format of much of al-Ilal suggests that many of its analyses originated in oral teaching sessions where students posed questions about specific hadith and ad-Daraqutni provided his assessments. This pedagogical origin gives the text a vivid, direct quality that a more formally composed treatise might lack.
